title: Get Out
logline: A young Black photographer's weekend trip to meet his white girlfriend's family in upstate New York spirals into a nightmare when he discovers their liberal facade conceals a horrifying conspiracy to exploit Black bodies.
antagonistOrConflict: The Armitage family and their social circle, who lure Black people to their estate to perform brain transplants that allow wealthy white buyers to inhabit young Black bodies
tone: Suspenseful, darkly satirical, increasingly claustrophobic with moments of dark humor that give way to visceral terror
themes: Racial exploitation and commodification of Black bodies
Loss of autonomy and bodily agency
Trust and betrayal in interracial relationships
The sunken place as metaphor for marginalization

actBreaks.description: Chris is hypnotized by Missy and sent to 'the sunken place' for the first time, marking his loss of control and the revelation that something is deeply wrong beyond social awkwardness

validation.ruling: Scene #57 (p37, 37%) is a valid Act 1 break: Missy hypnotizes Chris against his will, sending him to the Sunken Place. This irreversible violation fundamentally changes the story from awkward family visit to psychological horror, raises stakes dramatically, and forces Chris into new conflict where his autonomy has been stolen.

actBreaks.description: Chris attempts to leave but the family reveals themselves, Rose betrays him with the teacup, and he's sent back to the sunken place—the full conspiracy is exposed and he becomes a captive

validation.ruling: Scene #92 (p74, 74%) is a valid Act 2 break: The Armitage family traps Chris in the foyer as he tries to leave, Dean delivers his monologue about fire and the Sun God, and the family's facade completely drops. This point of no return forces Chris into direct survival mode and launches the final act's life-or-death confrontation.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script operates as a slow-burn horror that masquerades as social discomfort for its first half before revealing its true genre. The structure is deceptive by design—what feels like awkward 'meet the parents' comedy-drama steadily accumulates dread through micro-aggressions and unsettling details until the hypnosis scene (57) functions as a hinge, after which Chris can no longer deny something is wrong. The second act becomes investigative thriller as Chris pieces together the conspiracy during the garden party, culminating in Andre/Logan's violent break from his programming. The script then executes a devastating betrayal at scene 92 when Rose—his anchor and protector—is revealed as complicit. The final act is pure survival horror with Chris trapped and fighting his way out. The structure mirrors Chris's psychological journey from denial to awareness to action.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The opening 13 scenes establish the horror framework with Andre's abduction before shifting to Chris's seemingly unrelated story—a structural promise that these worlds will collide. The drive to the estate (scenes 27-36) uses the deer accident and cop encounter to build racial tension. Once at the estate, pacing slows deliberately through scenes 37-57, using uncomfortable social interactions and strange servant behavior to create sustained unease. The garden party sequence (scenes 63-70) accelerates with rapid-fire awkward encounters before the photo flash triggers Logan's breakdown—a sudden spike. After the hypnosis revelation, scenes become shorter and more fragmented (scenes 99-118 cover the escape in just 20 scenes), creating breathless momentum toward the climax.

incitingIncident.description: Chris calls Rod to confirm he'll watch Sid, Rod complains about work and jokes about elderly terrorists, Rose takes the phone to say hi, and Rod warns her to bring Chris back safely before they hang up and Rose hits a deer.

validation.ruling: Scene #28 is mundane car conversation. Scene #30 (p12, 12%) is the true inciting incident: Rose hits a deer with her car, shattering the ordinary road trip and forcing Chris into an unsettling encounter that establishes the script's racial horror themes and irreversible forward momentum into the Armitage family's sinister world.

midpoint.description: Chris calls Rod and describes being on display for the family friends and being hypnotized; Rod warns him about white people and sex slavery, referencing Jeffrey Dahmer, making Chris laugh but also consider the strangeness of his situation.

validation.ruling: Scene #63 shows party guests arriving, but Scene #78 (p58, 58%) is the midpoint: Chris discovers Rose's closet contains photos of her with multiple Black partners including Walter, Georgina, and Andre, revealing her complicity in a pattern of predation. This revelation shifts Chris from reactive confusion to proactive escape planning and represents the point of no return.

climax.description: Dean finishes removing Jim's skull cap and calls for Jeremy, then Chris charges from the darkness and impales Dean through the neck and shoulder with deer antlers, causing Dean to fall and knock over candles that set the bed on fire.

validation.ruling: Scene #112 shows Georgina's reaction in the kitchen. Scene #111 (p93, 93%) is the climax: Chris charges from darkness and impales Dean through the neck with deer antlers during the surgery, demonstrating his arc completion by using the deer imagery that haunted him, resolving the main conflict through direct confrontation at peak tension, and setting the bed on fire in the ultimate act of resistance.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE SHAW’S HOUSE - FRONT LAWN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Shaw family eats dinner inside their suburban home while discussing their upcoming Disney World trip.

scenes.heading: EXT. SUBURBAN STREET - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Andre, an African-American man, runs down a suburban sidewalk listening to jazz until his phone stops working, causing him to pause in front of the Shaw house.

scenes.heading: INT. THE SHAW’S HOUSE - DINING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Richard Shaw looks up from his tablet and watches Andre standing outside while the rest of his family continues their conversation about Disney.

scenes.heading: EXT. SUBURBAN STREET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Andre walks alone as darkness falls while a vintage cream-colored Porsche with tinted windows follows him down the street.

scenes.heading: INT. SPORTS CAR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: From inside the sports car, an unseen driver watches Andre while breathing heavily and listening to a French language tutorial.

scenes.heading: EXT. SUBURBAN STREET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Andre stops to tie his shoe and waves at the car, which also stops, then tries to communicate with the unseen driver who gives no response as the car continues to follow his movements.

scenes.heading: INT. SPORTS CAR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The driver watches Andre continue talking from inside the car while the French tutorial plays.

scenes.heading: EXT. SUBURBAN STREET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Andre confronts the car about stalking and harassment, banging on the window and demanding a response, but receives none.

scenes.heading: INT. SPORTS CAR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Andre bangs on the car window from outside.

scenes.heading: EXT. SUBURBAN STREET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The passenger window rolls down to reveal a driver wearing a medieval knight's helmet who raises a gun with a silencer and shoots Andre with a dart in the back as he tries to run.
scenes.turningPointNote: The opening sequence's threat becomes real violence — Andre is shot and the horror premise is established

scenes.heading: EXT. THE SHAW’S HOUSE - FRONT LAWN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Andre falls on the Shaw's front lawn with a dart in his back, reaching weakly toward the house for help before passing out as the masked driver approaches.

scenes.heading: INT. THE SHAW’S HOUSE - DINING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Shaw family continues eating dinner inside, unaware that the driver is carrying Andre's unconscious body from their front lawn.

scenes.heading: EXT. SUBURBAN STREET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The driver carries Andre to the car, places him in the padded trunk, and drives away.

scenes.heading: EXT. BROOKLYN PARK SLOPE - DAWN
scenes.summary: The sun rises over Brooklyn in autumn.

scenes.heading: INT. BROOKLYN LOFT - LIVING ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: The camera moves through a small, clean Brooklyn apartment decorated with urban photography.

scenes.heading: INT. BROOKLYN LOFT - BATHROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Chris Washington, shirtless, closes the medicine cabinet and scrutinizes his reflection in the bathroom mirror.

scenes.heading: INT. STARBUCKS COUNTER - MORNING
scenes.summary: Rose Armitage smiles while looking at pastries through the glass at a Starbucks counter.

scenes.heading: INT. BROOKLYN LOFT - BATHROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Chris shaves in the bathroom, postures a little, then nicks himself on the neck and smirks.

scenes.heading: INT. BROOKLYN LOFT - LIVING ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Chris looks through his camera at urban photographs while his dog Sid watches, then the buzzer rings.

scenes.heading: INT. BROOKLYN BUILDING - HALLWAY. MORNING
scenes.summary: Chris opens the door to find Rose holding two coffees and bags of pastries, takes the coffee tray, and pulls her inside to kiss.

scenes.heading: INT. BROOKLYN LOFT - LIVING ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Rose and Chris have coffee and pastries by the window while Rose pets Sid and they playfully discuss whether Chris neglects the dog, then Chris massages her foot.

scenes.heading: INT. BROOKLYN LOFT - CHRIS’ BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Chris packs for the weekend trip while Rose reminds him what to bring, then she grabs and breaks a cigarette from his mouth when he tries to smoke, and he admits he's nervous about meeting her parents.

scenes.heading: INT. BROOKLYN LOFT - LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Chris turns on the TV for Sid, then he and Rose say goodbye to the dog and leave the apartment.

scenes.heading: EXT. BROOKLYN NEIGHBORHOOD STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: Chris puts his luggage in the trunk and gets into the passenger side of Rose's white BMW.

scenes.heading: INT. BROOKLYN NIEGHBORHOOD STREET - PARKED CAR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Rose sits in the messy driver's seat, Chris teases her about it, she moves fast food wrappers to the back, takes a selfie of them together, and they drive off.

scenes.heading: EXT. NEW YORK - CITY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The car leaves New York City.

scenes.heading: EXT. RURAL ROAD - BIRD’S-EYE VIEW - DAY
scenes.summary: The car drives through beautiful countryside on a road flanked by woods.

scenes.heading: INT. ROSE’S CAR - DAY
scenes.summary: Chris asks Rose how long it's been since she visited home, tries to smoke a cigarette which Rose throws out the window, and Rose reveals she hasn't told her parents that Chris is Black.
scenes.turningPointNote: Chris learns Rose hasn't told her parents he's Black — a key revelation that introduces racial tension as a central concern

scenes.heading: EXT. LAGUARDIA AIRPORT - OUTSIDE THE TERMINAL - DAY
scenes.summary: Rod Williams, a TSA agent, smokes a cigarette outside LaGuardia Airport when his phone rings.

scenes.heading: INT. ROSE’S CAR - DAY
scenes.summary: Chris calls Rod to confirm he'll watch Sid, Rod complains about work and jokes about elderly terrorists, Rose takes the phone to say hi, and Rod warns her to bring Chris back safely before they hang up and Rose hits a deer.

scenes.heading: EXT. RURAL ROAD - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Rose and Chris get out to inspect the damage to her car, Chris walks toward the woods to check on the deer, and they hear a guttural moan of pain from the woods that stops when Chris approaches.

scenes.heading: EXT. UPSTATE NEW YORK ROAD - DAY
scenes.summary: A cop car arrives at the scene where the deer was hit, and Officer Crowe stands by Rose's car window while another officer stands near the deer.

scenes.heading: INT. ROSE’S CAR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Officer Crowe asks Chris for his ID even though Rose was driving, Rose refuses and argues it's racist profiling, the officer threatens to arrest her, Chris tries to calm the situation, and Rose tells the officer to fuck off before driving away.

scenes.heading: EXT. RURAL ROAD - DAY
scenes.summary: The car winds through a thickly wooded road.

scenes.heading: INT. ROSE’S CAR - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Chris watches Rose with new admiration after she defended him, she says she won't let anyone mess with her man, and announces they've arrived.

scenes.heading: EXT. ARMITAGE ESTATE - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Rose and Chris arrive at the isolated Armitage estate, see Walter the groundskeeper trimming hedges in the distance, wave to him, and Rose's parents Dean and Missy come out to greet them warmly.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMITAGE LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Dean welcomes Chris into the large, decorated living room filled with medieval art and taxidermy, Chris compliments the house, and Missy asks about their drive while Dean offers to show Chris around.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMITAGE UPSTAIRS HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Rose and Chris carry their bags upstairs.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMITAGE HOUSE - ROSE’S BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Rose and Chris enter her preserved childhood bedroom with pink decor and boy band posters, Chris looks at photos on her dresser including one from a high school production, and they discuss how great her parents are.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMITAGE DOWNSTAIRS HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Dean gives Chris a tour of the house showing family photos, mentions Rose's brother Jeremy is in med school, points out a photo of his father who lost to Jesse Owens in Olympic qualifying, and explains he would have won gold if he'd gone to Berlin.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMITAGE HOUSE - HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Dean tells Chris he's a retired neurosurgeon and mentions the basement has a black mold issue requiring everything to be thrown out.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMITAGE KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Dean and Chris enter the kitchen where Georgina, the African-American housekeeper, stands still facing a wall until she hears them and resumes cleaning with an eerie smile, then Dean opens the back door.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACKYARD - LATE AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Dean leads Chris through the huge backyard toward a gazebo, mentions the family loves games and total privacy, then addresses the awkwardness of having Black servants by explaining he and Missy hired them to care for his parents and kept them on after his parents died.

scenes.heading: EXT. OUTDOOR PATIO - DAY
scenes.summary: On the patio, Missy asks Chris about his deceased parents, Chris explains his mother was hit by a car when he was eleven and his aunt raised him, Missy asks if they're in love, and Dean comments on how Walter is in better shape than he was at his age.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMITAGE DINING ROOM - LATER/NIGHT
scenes.summary: At dinner, Jeremy drunkenly tells embarrassing stories about Rose including one where he beat up a guy she had a crush on, demonstrates a martial arts chokehold on Chris while saying he could have stopped him despite Chris being in better shape, and Dean intervenes to stop him.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE ESTATE. NIGHT
scenes.summary: Night falls over the estate under a full moon with crickets chirping.

scenes.heading: INT. ROSE’S BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rose and Chris make love quietly in her bed while a stuffed lion seems to watch, prompting Chris to turn it away.

scenes.heading: INT. ROSE’S BEDROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Rose sleeps while Chris lies awake, swats at a buzzing in his ear, hears wind rush through the room as the closet door creaks open, and his eyes drift to cigarettes in his camera bag.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMITAGE HALLWAY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Chris leaves Rose's room and walks down the dark hallway where a floorboard creaks under his feet, then turns down the stairs.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMITAGE DOWNSTAIRS HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Chris reaches the bottom of the stairs, hears the floorboard creak upstairs and turns expecting someone but sees nothing, walks past family photos toward the kitchen, peers curiously into the open basement door revealing pitch darkness, closes it, and continues walking unaware of a figure now standing at the end of the hallway behind him.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMITAGE KITCHEN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Chris walks through the kitchen, bumps a bar chair slightly, and exits through the sliding back door.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACKYARD - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Chris steps outside to smoke and sees something darting across the yard; Walter the groundskeeper runs past him from the darkness, startling Chris before the kitchen light turns on.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMITAGE KITCHEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Georgina examines her teeth in the window's reflection, unaware of Chris outside.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACKYARD - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Chris realizes he hasn't been spotted; Georgina notices the moved bar chair, repositions it, and begins cleaning while Chris sneaks around the house.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMITAGE DINING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Chris sneaks into the dark dining room.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMITAGE LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Chris moves toward the stairs when a lamp turns on, revealing Missy sitting nearby with her cat; she catches him and offers to help him quit smoking.

scenes.heading: INT. MISSY’S OFFICE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Missy pours tea and begins stirring rhythmically with a spoon while Chris sits across from her; she guides him into a hypnotic state as he protests wanting to stop, but she continues the process.
scenes.turningPointNote: Chris is hypnotized against his will for the first time, establishing Missy's control and the family's sinister methods

scenes.heading: INT. DARKNESS
scenes.summary: Chris falls through darkness in a hypnotic state while his physical body sits motionless with eyes wide open in Missy's office.

scenes.heading: INT. DARKNESS
scenes.summary: Chris continues falling through darkness, screaming to be brought back; he lands in a deep place and looks up at a distant screen showing Missy standing over his paralyzed body.

scenes.heading: INT. ROSE’S BEDROOM - DAWN
scenes.summary: Chris wakes up sweaty and confused in Rose's bed at dawn; he receives a text from Rod with a photo and smiles before plugging in his low-battery phone.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACKYARD - DAWN
scenes.summary: Chris walks through the yard photographing nature; he sees Georgina through a window removing her wig, then is startled by a bird hitting the gazebo and by Walter standing fifty feet away smiling at him.

scenes.heading: INT. ROSE’S BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Chris tells Rose he thinks her mother hypnotized him last night and had disturbing dreams; Rose reveals she had nightmares too when hypnotized as a child, then Chris asks about the help and Rose explains her parents kept them on after her grandparents died.

scenes.heading: EXT. FRONT YARD - NOON
scenes.summary: Walter directs twelve expensive cars onto the front lawn as wealthy guests emerge from each vehicle.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACKYARD - NOON
scenes.summary: Chris and Rose mingle at the party with thirty white guests and one Japanese man; Missy winks at Chris, and Gordon and Emily Greene stop them to talk while watching Chris with fascination.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACKYARD - LATER
scenes.summary: Chris and Rose speak with Nelson and Lisa Deets; Lisa squeezes Chris's bicep and asks Rose if the lovemaking is better, making Chris nearly choke on his drink.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACKYARD - LATER
scenes.summary: Chris and Rose talk to Parker and April Dray about race; Chris notices Dean pointing him out to couples who wave and smile giddily.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACKYARD - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Chris approaches Andre at the bar; Andre speaks in a completely different voice with no urban dialect, speaking slowly and precisely, then Phil possessively takes Andre away.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACKYARD - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Chris returns to Rose agitated as Dean begins a speech thanking everyone for gathering and comparing it to knights gathering for a crusade.

scenes.heading: EXT. GAZEBO. DAY.
scenes.summary: Chris wanders to the gazebo where Jim Hudson, a blind art dealer, criticizes the other guests as ignorant and reveals he's an admirer of Chris's photography work.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACKYARD - LATER
scenes.summary: Chris plays badminton with Rose against Jeremy and Derrick; after Jeremy trash-talks him, Chris serves hard and wins a long rally that captivates the crowd.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMITAGE HOUSE - KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Chris walks through the kitchen.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMITAGE LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Chris slips past mingling guests and goes upstairs; as soon as he's out of sight, all the guests stop their fake conversations and listen to his footsteps above.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMITAGE UPSTAIRS HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Chris walks to Rose's room.

scenes.heading: INT. ROSE’S BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Chris exits the bathroom and finds his phone unplugged and dead despite leaving it charging; he plugs it in again and hears a creak down the hall.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMITAGE UPSTAIRS HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Chris peers into Jeremy's room and sees Georgina making the bed; he quickly retreats when the door creaks and she turns, meeting Rose in the hallway.

scenes.heading: INT. ROSE’S BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Chris tells Rose that Georgina unplugged his phone; Rose dismisses it as an honest mistake while Chris suggests Georgina doesn't like him being with Rose, leading to a tense exchange.

scenes.heading: INT. CHRIS’ APARTMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: Rod eats a cheeseburger watching TV with Sid when his phone rings.

scenes.heading: INT. ROSE’S ROOM - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Chris calls Rod and describes being on display for the family friends and being hypnotized; Rod warns him about white people and sex slavery, referencing Jeffrey Dahmer, making Chris laugh but also consider the strangeness of his situation.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACKYARD - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Dean introduces Chris to seven people who all seem to share a private joke; Hiroki asks Chris in Japanese if he can ask questions, then asks whether being African American has more advantages or disadvantages in the modern world.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Chris takes a flash photo of Andre/Logan, who suddenly lunges at Chris screaming "Get out!" before collapsing; Dean explains it was a seizure triggered by the camera flash.
scenes.turningPointNote: Andre's violent outburst and warning to Chris escalates the threat and confirms something is deeply wrong

scenes.heading: EXT. LAKESIDE - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Chris tells Rose that what happened wasn't a seizure and that he felt like he knew Andre from somewhere.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACK YARD. SAME TIME
scenes.summary: With Chris and Rose away, the party guests gather silently facing Dean who stands by a large picture of Chris on an easel; Dean makes hand signals and guests raise their hands in an auction.
scenes.turningPointNote: The revelation that Chris is being auctioned confirms the conspiracy and his mortal danger

scenes.heading: EXT. LAKESIDE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Chris tells Rose he wants to leave tonight because something doesn't feel right; Rose resists, saying it's a big deal and she wouldn't know what to tell her family.

scenes.heading: INT. BACKYARD - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Dean continues the auction with hand signals; Gordon Greene raises his hand and Dean accepts his bid.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAKESIDE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Rose tells Chris she's late for her period and might be pregnant; Chris struggles to respond emotionally, and Rose becomes upset at his lack of reaction.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACKYARD - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: The auction intensifies between three couples; Jim Hudson signals ten with both hands and wins after no one challenges his bid, with Dean banging his fist to close the auction.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAKESIDE - DUSK
scenes.summary: Chris and Rose sit by the lake at sunset; Chris tells her about the night his mother died, how he didn't call 911, and how she survived the initial hit but died alone by the roadside.

scenes.heading: EXT. FRONT LAWN - DUSK
scenes.summary: Chris and Rose approach the house as the last guests drive away; Lisa Deets drunkenly says goodbye while Walter watches the cars leave and turns to Chris with a smile.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMITAGE BATHROOM - DUSK
scenes.summary: Chris washes his hands and receives a text from Rod with a picture of witches sacrificing a man; Chris sends Rod the photo of Andre/Logan he took earlier.

scenes.heading: INT. CHRIS’ APARTMENT - DUSK
scenes.summary: Rod identifies the photo as Andre Hayworth, who used to date Veronica and was street but is now different and seemingly white-acting; Rod insists Andre wasn't gay and tells Chris to get out.

scenes.heading: INT. ROSE’S BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Chris packs his bag and sees Jim Hudson's driver deliver a green envelope to Missy before leaving without Jim; Chris finds a red shoebox in Rose's closet containing photos of her with multiple Black partners including Walter, Georgina, and Andre.
scenes.turningPointNote: Chris discovers Rose has been complicit all along, shattering his trust and confirming the full scope of the conspiracy

scenes.heading: INT. FOYER/LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Chris comes downstairs with Rose following; Jeremy blocks the door with his lacrosse stick while Dean and Missy sit in the living room; Rose claims to know where the keys are, but Dean starts playing classical music and begins conducting it.

scenes.heading: INT. DARKNESS
scenes.summary: Chris falls through the abyss into the Sunken Place again; the screen above shows his perspective falling to the floor and crashing, with Jeremy and the family standing over him discussing whether he's hurt.

scenes.heading: INT. AIRPORT - SECURITY CHECK - DAY
scenes.summary: Rod sits distracted at airport security until his supervisor Gary snaps fingers in his face and tells him to take a break.

scenes.heading: EXT. AIRPORT - DEPARTURES TERMINAL - DAY
scenes.summary: Rod smokes outside the airport and calls Chris, leaving a voicemail asking if he's home and to let him know he's okay.

scenes.heading: INT. CHRIS’ LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rod opens Chris's apartment door and Sid wags his tail hungrily.

scenes.heading: INT. CHRIS’ KITCHEN - LATER
scenes.summary: Rod opens dog food while calling Chris and getting voicemail; he places Sid's bowl down but Sid doesn't eat and instead whines at Rod.

scenes.heading: INT. CHRIS’ LIVING ROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Rod searches "Andre Hayworth" on Chris's laptop and finds an article titled "What Happened to Andre Hayworth?" about a Brooklyn native going missing in Evergreen Hallow.
scenes.turningPointNote: Rod discovers Andre is a missing person, confirming the danger Chris is in and launching the rescue effort

scenes.heading: INT. GAMES ROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Chris wakes up strapped to a leather chair in a dark games room with mounted animal heads; he pulls at the straps and screams for Rose and to be let out.

scenes.heading: INT. CHRIS’ LIVING ROOM - DAWN
scenes.summary: Sid wakes Rod up with a lick to the face on Chris's sofa at dawn.

scenes.heading: INT. POLICE STATION - DAY
scenes.summary: Rod sits in Detective Latoya's office with his dog Sid and begins to explain his concerns about Chris's disappearance while Latoya eats cashews and tells him TSA issues should go to his authorizing officer.

scenes.heading: INT. POLICE STATION - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Rod explains his theory about hypnosis and missing Black men to three detectives who laugh at him and dismiss his concerns entirely, treating it as a joke about white girls.

scenes.heading: INT. GAMES ROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Chris, strapped to a chair and too weak to struggle, asks if the song playing on repeat is the only one they have.

scenes.heading: INT. GAMES ROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Chris hears the song end and begin again, then desperately asks what they want and says he'll do what they say if they just answer him.

scenes.heading: INT. CHRIS’ LOFT - DUSK
scenes.summary: Rod sits in Chris's loft with Sid, calls Chris's phone again, and is shocked when Rose answers instead.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMITAGE HOUSE. DINING ROOM - DUSK
scenes.summary: Rose tells Rod that Chris left two days ago after a fight and took a cab home without his phone, then Rod reveals he went to the police and begins recording her on Garage Band while questioning her about her mother hitting a deer.
scenes.turningPointNote: Rod confirms his suspicions about Rose's involvement and begins gathering evidence by recording their conversation.

scenes.heading: INT. GAMES ROOM
scenes.summary: Chris sings along to "You've Got A Friend" in resignation until the song ends and the television flickers to show a new image.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL ROOM - DAY (TELEVISION)
scenes.summary: Jim Hudson appears on the television and explains to Chris that he needs to understand what's happening to raise the success rate of the procedure, reveals Rose was bait and Jeremy's method is less pleasant, and describes the Coagula procedure where Chris's consciousness will be suppressed in the sunken place while Jim takes over his body.
scenes.turningPointNote: The full horror of the Armitage family's plan is revealed to Chris through Jim's explanation of the brain transplant procedure.

scenes.heading: INT. OPERATING ROOM.
scenes.summary: Jim Hudson lies unconscious on an operating bed with earbuds playing the song and his skull marked for surgery while Dean prays in a robe and Jeremy watches, unaware of events elsewhere.

scenes.heading: INT. DARK HALLWAY
scenes.summary: Jeremy exits a room and walks down a hallway, finds Rose's body in the games room, then Chris emerges from darkness and beats him repeatedly with a badminton racket.
scenes.turningPointNote: Chris escapes his restraints and begins violently fighting back against his captors.

scenes.heading: INT. OPERATING ROOM
scenes.summary: Dean finishes removing Jim's skull cap and calls for Jeremy, then Chris charges from the darkness and impales Dean through the neck and shoulder with deer antlers, causing Dean to fall and knock over candles that set the bed on fire.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Missy pours tea in the kitchen while Georgina knits nearby, then Missy sees Chris's bloody reflection in the window behind her and Georgina screams and runs out the back door while Chris goes for his phone.

scenes.heading: INT. DINING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Chris grabs his phone from the dining room table as it powers on, Missy appears behind him clinking her teacup, Chris knocks the cup from her hand and punches the cat out of the air, then Missy grabs a knife from the kitchen.

scenes.heading: INT. FOYER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Chris runs toward the front door but Jeremy bursts from the dining room and puts him in a chokehold, Chris slams his head back into Jeremy's face repeatedly, reaches into Jeremy's pocket for keys, and stabs Jeremy in the leg with them.

scenes.heading: EXT. FRONT YARD - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Chris runs outside, sees the beige sports car from the opening scene, looks at Jeremy downed in the doorway, and decides to take the car.

scenes.heading: INT. SPORTS CAR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Chris drives away in the sports car while calling 911, accidentally hits Georgina in the darkness, stops and debates leaving her, then reverses back and puts her in the passenger seat where she suddenly attacks him and causes the car to crash into a tree.
scenes.turningPointNote: Chris's escape is derailed when Georgina attacks him in the car, leading to a crash that leaves him vulnerable again.

scenes.heading: INT. MAXIMUM SECURITY PRISON. VISITING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rod visits Chris in prison where Chris, now bearded and in a jumpsuit, smokes a cigarette and repeatedly tells Rod he doesn't remember the names Rod needs to prove the conspiracy.

scenes.heading: INT. MAXIMUM SECURITY PRISON. HALLWAY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Two guards escort Chris back to his cell while he whistles "You Got A Friend."
